overview: Add password-based access control to the web terminal and track recently used working directories for quick session creation. The security module ensures only authorized users can access the terminal via a password set on first visit, stored as a bcrypt hash on the server. The recent directories feature records the working directory each time a session is created or a tmux session is attached, and surfaces the last 10 unique paths in the session creation wizard.
goals: Protect the web terminal with a password gate so unauthorized users cannot access any functionality
Persist authentication state in the browser for 30 days to avoid repeated password entry
Secure both HTTP API endpoints and WebSocket connections
Record working directory paths on every session creation and tmux attach
Display the 10 most recent unique directory paths in the session creation wizard for quick selection
nonGoals: Rate limiting or brute-force protection (can be added later)
Syncing recent directories across different server instances
successMetrics: First-time visitors are prompted to set a password before accessing any feature
Returning visitors on new browsers must enter the password to gain access
Same browser does not require re-authentication within 30 days
All HTTP endpoints and WebSocket connections reject unauthenticated requests
Recent directories list shows up to 10 entries in the session creation wizard
All quality gates pass: node -c server.js and npx playwright test

stack.auth: bcrypt password hash stored in server-side JSON config, token-based auth via localStorage

routes.name: Login
routes.purpose: Verify password and return auth token

routes.name: Auth Status
routes.purpose: Check if password is set and if current token is valid

routes.name: Change Password
routes.purpose: Change password with old password verification

routes.name: Recent Dirs
routes.purpose: Return the 10 most recent unique directory paths

uiNotes: Auth overlay: full-screen modal overlay with centered card, blurred background, matching cyberpunk theme
Two modes for auth overlay: setup mode (password + confirm password + set button) and login mode (password + login button)
Change password: accessible from a settings icon in the toolbar, opens a modal with old/new/confirm fields
Recent dirs: shown as a clickable list above the directory browser in wizard step 0, with directory path and relative timestamp
Recent dirs list items should be single-click to select (same as directory browser behavior)

fields: entries (array of {path, timestamp}, max 50 stored, 10 displayed)

rules: Password must be at least 6 characters
Password is hashed with bcrypt (cost factor 10) before storage
Auth token is a crypto.randomUUID(), stored in localStorage on client and validated on server
Token expires 30 days after creation; server prunes expired tokens on each validation
If no password is set (config.json missing or empty passwordHash), show setup overlay
If password is set but no valid token in localStorage, show login overlay
All API endpoints except /api/auth/* require valid auth token in Authorization header
WebSocket connections require valid auth token as query parameter (?token=xxx)
Recent dirs are stored server-side in recent-dirs.json, shared across all browsers
Each session creation or tmux attach appends {path, timestamp} to recent-dirs.json
Duplicate paths update the timestamp instead of creating new entries
Recent dirs API returns the 10 most recently used unique paths, sorted by timestamp descending
Config files are stored in the project data directory (e.g., .web-terminal/ in project root or configurable)

stories.title: Server-side config file infrastructure
stories.description: As a developer, I want a config file module that reads/writes JSON config files so that the auth and recent-dirs features have persistent storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a config module (e.g., lib/config.js) that manages reading/writing JSON files in a .web-terminal/ data directory
Data directory defaults to <project-root>/.web-terminal/ and is auto-created if missing
Provides getConfig()/saveConfig() for config.json and getRecentDirs()/saveRecentDirs() for recent-dirs.json
File writes are atomic (write to temp file, then rename) to prevent corruption
Example: getConfig() returns {} when config.json doesn't exist yet
Negative case: malformed JSON in config file is handled gracefully (returns defaults, logs warning)
Install bcryptjs: npm install bcryptjs
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Password setup API endpoint
stories.description: As a first-time user, I want to set an access password so that subsequent visits require authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/setup accepts { password, confirmPassword } in request body
Validates password >= 6 characters and password === confirmPassword
Rejects if a password is already set (returns 409 Conflict)
Hashes password with bcrypt (cost 10) and stores in config.json as passwordHash
Generates an auth token (crypto.randomUUID), stores it in config.json authTokens array with createdAt timestamp
Returns { token } on success
Example: POST with { password: 'mypass1', confirmPassword: 'mypass1' } → 200 { token: 'uuid...' }
Negative case: POST with { password: 'ab', confirmPassword: 'ab' } → 400 { error: 'Password must be at least 6 characters' }
Negative case: POST with { password: 'mypass1', confirmPassword: 'mypass2' } → 400 { error: 'Passwords do not match' }
Negative case: POST when password already set → 409 { error: 'Password already configured' }
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Login API endpoint
stories.description: As a returning user on a new browser, I want to enter my password to get an auth token so that I can access the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/login accepts { password } in request body
Compares password against stored bcrypt hash using bcryptjs.compare()
On success: generates new auth token, adds to authTokens array with createdAt, returns { token }
On failure: returns 401 { error: 'Invalid password' }
Prunes expired tokens (older than 30 days) from authTokens array on each login
Example: correct password → 200 { token: 'uuid...' }
Negative case: wrong password → 401 { error: 'Invalid password' }
Negative case: no password set yet → 400 { error: 'Password not configured' }
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Auth status endpoint and auth middleware
stories.description: As the frontend app, I need to check auth status on load and protect all other endpoints with auth middleware.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/auth/status returns { passwordSet: boolean, authenticated: boolean }
Auth status checks Authorization header for 'Bearer <token>' and validates token against config.json authTokens
Expired tokens (older than 30 days) are treated as invalid
Auth middleware function rejects requests without valid token with 401 status
Auth middleware is applied to ALL existing API routes EXCEPT /api/auth/*
Example: GET /api/auth/status with valid token → { passwordSet: true, authenticated: true }
Example: GET /api/auth/status without token and password is set → { passwordSet: true, authenticated: false }
Example: GET /api/auth/status without token and no password → { passwordSet: false, authenticated: false }
Negative case: GET /api/sessions without token → 401 { error: 'Authentication required' }
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: WebSocket authentication
stories.description: As a security measure, I want WebSocket connections to require a valid auth token so that unauthenticated users cannot access terminal sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Both lobby WebSocket and terminal WebSocket connections require ?token=<authToken> query parameter
On connection, server validates the token against config.json authTokens
If token is missing or invalid, server sends an error control message and closes the WebSocket with code 4001
If no password is configured yet (first-time setup), WebSocket connections are allowed without token
Frontend passes the stored auth token in WebSocket URL: ws://host?session=<id>&token=<token>
Example: valid token in query → connection established normally
Negative case: no token in query when password is set → WebSocket closed with 4001
Negative case: expired token → WebSocket closed with 4001
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Change password API endpoint
stories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to change my password so that I can maintain security.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/change-password accepts { oldPassword, newPassword, confirmPassword }
Requires valid auth token (uses auth middleware)
Verifies oldPassword against stored hash
Validates newPassword >= 6 characters and newPassword === confirmPassword
Updates passwordHash in config.json with new bcrypt hash
Invalidates all existing tokens except the current one
Returns { success: true }
Example: valid old password + valid new password → 200 { success: true }
Negative case: wrong old password → 401 { error: 'Current password is incorrect' }
Negative case: new password too short → 400 { error: 'Password must be at least 6 characters' }
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Frontend auth overlay — setup and login UI
stories.description: As a user, I want to see a password setup or login screen when I'm not authenticated so that I can access the terminal securely.
acceptanceCriteria: On app load, frontend calls GET /api/auth/status to determine auth state
If passwordSet is false: show full-screen overlay with 'Set Access Password' form (password + confirm password + submit button)
If passwordSet is true and authenticated is false: show full-screen overlay with 'Enter Password' form (password + submit button)
If authenticated is true: hide overlay, proceed to normal app
Auth overlay covers the entire page with a backdrop blur, styled to match the cyberpunk theme
Setup form: on submit, calls POST /api/auth/setup, stores returned token in localStorage as 'authToken', reloads app state
Login form: on submit, calls POST /api/auth/login, stores returned token in localStorage, reloads app state
Error messages are shown inline below the form (e.g., 'Passwords do not match', 'Invalid password')
Password fields have type='password' for masking
Example: first visit → setup overlay → set password → overlay disappears, app loads
Example: new browser → login overlay → enter password → overlay disappears
Negative case: wrong password → inline error message, form stays open
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Frontend change password modal
stories.description: As an authenticated user, I want a UI to change my password from the toolbar.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a settings/lock icon button in the toolbar area
Clicking it opens a modal with fields: current password, new password, confirm new password
On submit, calls POST /api/auth/change-password with Authorization header
On success: shows success message, closes modal
On failure: shows inline error message
Modal is styled consistently with the existing cyberpunk theme and wizard modal
Example: change password successfully → success toast/message, modal closes
Negative case: wrong current password → inline error, modal stays open
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Server-side recent directories tracking
stories.description: As the system, I want to record the working directory each time a session is created or a tmux session is attached so that users can quickly revisit recent directories.
acceptanceCriteria: On POST /api/sessions (create session): record the cwd from request body to recent-dirs.json
On POST /api/attach/tmux (attach tmux): record the tmux session's working directory to recent-dirs.json
For tmux attach, get working directory via tmux display-message -p '#{pane_current_path}' or similar
Each entry is { path: string, timestamp: ISO8601 string }
If the path already exists in the list, update its timestamp instead of adding a duplicate
Store up to 50 entries (to have history beyond the displayed 10)
GET /api/recent-dirs returns the 10 most recent unique entries sorted by timestamp descending
GET /api/recent-dirs requires auth middleware
Example: create session with cwd '/home/user/project-a' → entry added with current timestamp
Example: create another session with same cwd → timestamp updated, no duplicate entry
Negative case: create session without cwd (undefined) → no entry recorded
node -c server.js passes

stories.title: Frontend recent directories in session wizard
stories.description: As a user, I want to see my recently used directories in the session creation wizard so that I can quickly select a familiar working directory.
acceptanceCriteria: In wizard step 0 (directory selection), fetch GET /api/recent-dirs on wizard open
Display a 'Recent Directories' section above the directory browser tree
Show up to 10 entries with the directory path and a relative time label (e.g., '2 hours ago', 'yesterday')
Single-click on a recent dir entry selects it as the working directory (same behavior as clicking in the dir browser)
Selected recent dir is visually highlighted
If no recent dirs exist, the section is hidden (no empty state clutter)
Section styled consistently with existing wizard modal theme
Example: user sees '/home/user/project-a — 2 hours ago', clicks it → wizard.selectedDir is set, path bar updates
Negative case: fresh install with no history → recent dirs section not shown
node -c server.js passes
npx playwright test passes
